ogTitle: Te Kowai vs Teddington: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Te Kowai and Teddington.
heading: Te Kowai vs Teddington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Te Kowai, Queensland and Teddington, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Te Kowai has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Teddington (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Te Kowai or Teddington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Te Kowai has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Teddington scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Te Kowai scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Teddington scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Te Kowai or Teddington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Te Kowai has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.53% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
